name: vacuous_stable_any_pred
code:
proof fn phi_5_vacuous_stable_any_pred(p: TempPred<int>, q: TempPred<int>, ex: Execution<int>)
    requires
        valid(stable(p.leads_to(q))),
        !p.leads_to(q).satisfied_by(ex),
    ensures
        stable(p.leads_to(q)).satisfied_by(ex),
{
}
reason: stable(X) = X ==> always(X), so when X is false the implication is vacuously true — stable is trivially satisfied for any execution where the predicate fails, which means valid(stable(X)) gives no information about executions where X doesn't hold
